_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, including paper writing, conference presentation, and internal briefing formats.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. Skill LS-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-011 (AI, cosine: 0.469), OP-S-004 (OP, cosine: 0.456).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.594). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. NICE-only terms [information, summarizing] vs WIDAI-only terms [audiences, briefing, communicating, conference, findings, formats] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.907, R=0.855). P exceeds R by 0.052 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Skill in summarizing information" and "Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical aud" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.479 < STS 0.594 (gap -0.115). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [information, summarizing] and [audiences, briefing, communicating] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.5%. Shared tokens: [skill]. NICE-only: [information, summarizing]. WIDAI-only: [audiences, briefing, communicating, conference, findings, formats].
